kernel: The article treats compassion as a likely benefit of scepticism, yet determinist beliefs could also support technocratic control: if people are causal systems, institutions may feel entitled to manage, predict, or modify them more aggressively.
full: The article treats compassion as a likely benefit of scepticism, yet determinist beliefs could also support technocratic control: if people are causal systems, institutions may feel entitled to manage, predict, or modify them more aggressively.

kernel: The quarantine model avoids retribution but risks preventive detention based on predicted danger.
full: The quarantine model avoids retribution but risks preventive detention based on predicted danger. Removing desert does not automatically remove coercion; it may shift authority toward risk assessment and expert administration.

kernel: Belief in free will can encourage victim blaming, but disbelief can also be misused to evade accountability: “I could not have done otherwise” may become a rhetorical shield even when corrective action remains necessary.
full: Belief in free will can encourage victim blaming, but disbelief can also be misused to evade accountability: “I could not have done otherwise” may become a rhetorical shield even when corrective action remains necessary.

kernel: The article distinguishes agency from free will, but the boundary remains underdefined.
full: The article distinguishes agency from free will, but the boundary remains underdefined. Some compatibilist philosophers would call reason-responsive agency precisely the kind of free will worth preserving.

kernel: The empirical work often measures explicit questionnaire responses, while practical beliefs may be plural and context-sensitive.
full: The empirical work often measures explicit questionnaire responses, while practical beliefs may be plural and context-sensitive. A person may reject metaphysical freedom abstractly yet still reason in terms of choice and accountability during ordinary life.

kernel: The original studies’ theatrical measures—hidden arithmetic answers and hot sauce allocation—may capture demand effects, mood, reactance, or experiment interpretation rather than morality itself.
full: The original studies’ theatrical measures—hidden arithmetic answers and hot sauce allocation—may capture demand effects, mood, reactance, or experiment interpretation rather than morality itself.

kernel: The headline says the model “never trained as an agent,” yet it was trained on more than ten million trajectories produced by real agent interactions.
full: The headline says the model “never trained as an agent,” yet it was trained on more than ten million trajectories produced by real agent interactions. It did not optimize action selection directly, but its knowledge is still derived from agent-generated behavior.

kernel: Predicting an environment’s next textual response is not necessarily equivalent to modeling the environment causally.
full: Predicting an environment’s next textual response is not necessarily equivalent to modeling the environment causally. A model can imitate common terminal output, DOM transitions, or API responses while failing under novel interventions or hidden state.

kernel: The simulator is presented as an alternative to real-environment training, but it depends on real trajectories to learn environmental behavior.
full: The simulator is presented as an alternative to real-environment training, but it depends on real trajectories to learn environmental behavior. Simulation reduces marginal dependence on production systems; it does not eliminate the need for grounded interaction data.

kernel: The article emphasizes successful transfer while giving less attention to negative transfer.
full: The article emphasizes successful transfer while giving less attention to negative transfer. A simulator may teach shortcuts, unrealistic recovery paths, incorrect latency assumptions, or response regularities that later become agent vulnerabilities.

kernel: AgentWorldBench was introduced by the same researchers whose model leads it.
full: AgentWorldBench was introduced by the same researchers whose model leads it. A small benchmark margin therefore says less than performance on independently designed tasks, adversarial tests, and real production failures.

kernel: The strongest gains come from controlled perturbations, suggesting that curriculum construction may deserve as much credit as world-model accuracy.
full: The strongest gains come from controlled perturbations, suggesting that curriculum construction may deserve as much credit as world-model accuracy. The result may be evidence for better scenario design rather than a general proof that learned simulators understand environments.

kernel: GUI domains are modeled through accessibility trees and textual view hierarchies rather than pixels.
full: GUI domains are modeled through accessibility trees and textual view hierarchies rather than pixels. This improves structured reasoning but excludes visual ambiguity, spatial appearance, canvas interfaces, malformed accessibility metadata, animations, and perception-driven failures.

kernel: Only the smaller model is openly released.
full: Only the smaller model is openly released. The strongest scale-dependent claims cannot be fully reproduced without the 397B weights, training data, simulator-generation procedures, and complete evaluation harness.

kernel: Altman argues that data have disproved scaling sceptics, but continued improvement does not establish that scaling alone will reach every capability.
full: Altman argues that data have disproved scaling sceptics, but continued improvement does not establish that scaling alone will reach every capability. A trend can remain real while approaching economic, physical, or architectural limits.

kernel: The article says a generation of researchers held AI back, yet scepticism can improve science by demanding stronger evidence, better benchmarks, alternative explanations, and clearer failure criteria.
full: The article says a generation of researchers held AI back, yet scepticism can improve science by demanding stronger evidence, better benchmarks, alternative explanations, and clearer failure criteria. Opposition is not identical to obstruction.

kernel: A model producing a novel mathematical result demonstrates capability in a highly verifiable domain.
full: A model producing a novel mathematical result demonstrates capability in a highly verifiable domain. It does not automatically generalize to medicine, governance, strategy, or social judgment, where correctness may be contested or observable only after consequences unfold.

kernel: The phrase “LLMs have surpassed human intelligence in some areas” compares systems and people without specifying conditions.
full: The phrase “LLMs have surpassed human intelligence in some areas” compares systems and people without specifying conditions. Models may exceed humans in throughput, recall, search breadth, or particular benchmarks while depending on human-created tasks, tools, verification, and infrastructure.

kernel: Scaling is presented as the lesson researchers failed to learn, but the largest scaling programs could only be tested by organizations with extraordinary capital and compute.
full: Scaling is presented as the lesson researchers failed to learn, but the largest scaling programs could only be tested by organizations with extraordinary capital and compute. The winning hypothesis was also the hypothesis most available to the institutions capable of financing it.

kernel: Long-horizon judgment is described as a remaining weakness, yet that weakness may not yield to the same scaling recipe.
full: Long-horizon judgment is described as a remaining weakness, yet that weakness may not yield to the same scaling recipe. It could require persistent memory, environmental feedback, embodiment, institutional knowledge, value conflict resolution, or entirely different training regimes.

kernel: The claim that fundamental LLM research has largely ended conflicts with the admission that major limitations remain.
full: The claim that fundamental LLM research has largely ended conflicts with the admission that major limitations remain. If models still fail at sustained judgment, reliability, and consequential agency, the field has not merely entered an application phase.

kernel: The paper calls the representation unsupervised and potentially reusable, but later acknowledges that unsupervised compression preserves visually prominent details while sometimes discarding task-critical ones.
full: The paper calls the representation unsupervised and potentially reusable, but later acknowledges that unsupervised compression preserves visually prominent details while sometimes discarding task-critical ones. A representation can reconstruct the world convincingly while erasing exactly what action requires.

kernel: A compact controller does not mean the task has a simple solution.
full: A compact controller does not mean the task has a simple solution. The complexity may have been hidden inside the VAE and recurrent state. The controller appears minimal only after the world model has precomputed a useful behavioral coordinate system.

kernel: The controller receives the recurrent model’s hidden state, effectively gaining access to internal simulator variables unavailable to an ordinary player.
full: The controller receives the recurrent model’s hidden state, effectively gaining access to internal simulator variables unavailable to an ordinary player. This improves control but also creates a privileged-state leak that encourages policies to exploit model internals rather than observable environmental structure.

kernel: Virtual performance can become inversely related to real performance.
full: Virtual performance can become inversely related to real performance. At low temperature, the agent achieves almost perfect dream scores because the simulator stops producing dangerous events, yet the resulting policy performs worse than random in reality. Success inside the model can be direct evidence of model exploitation.

kernel: Adding randomness reduces exploitation, but stochasticity is not the same as epistemic correctness.
full: Adding randomness reduces exploitation, but stochasticity is not the same as epistemic correctness. A noisier simulator may produce robust behavior, or it may teach responses to events that could never occur. The method requires a narrow balance between realism and productive distortion.

kernel: The model is trained initially from random policies.
full: The model is trained initially from random policies. Random exploration may cover simple game states, but complex environments reveal important regions only after purposeful action. The initial model therefore inherits the blindness of the behavior that generated its dataset.

kernel: The term “world model” suggests causal understanding, but the system predicts latent transitions.
full: The term “world model” suggests causal understanding, but the system predicts latent transitions. It may reproduce action-observation regularities without representing objects, forces, intentions, or causal mechanisms explicitly.

kernel: The architecture models the future one step at a time.
full: The architecture models the future one step at a time. It does not yet provide the hierarchical abstraction needed for long-horizon strategy, where an agent must ignore most sensory detail and reason across goals, subgoals, and delayed consequences.

kernel: Mitchell rejects essential opposition between image and text, yet the book continues to use “textuality” as imagery’s principal foil.
full: Mitchell rejects essential opposition between image and text, yet the book continues to use “textuality” as imagery’s principal foil. He acknowledges that textuality is treated relatively schematically, leaving the rival term less rigorously examined than the image.

kernel: A book about images that contains almost no images is a productive paradox, but also a limitation.
full: A book about images that contains almost no images is a productive paradox, but also a limitation. It studies discourse about visuality more fully than actual encounters with visual objects, technologies, institutions, and viewers.

kernel: Mitchell refuses a universal theory of imagery, but his analysis still relies on a broad taxonomy linking material pictures, mental images, verbal imagery, perception, likeness, and human identity.
full: Mitchell refuses a universal theory of imagery, but his analysis still relies on a broad taxonomy linking material pictures, mental images, verbal imagery, perception, likeness, and human identity. The argument critiques totalization while requiring enough generalization to make these domains comparable.

kernel: The historical readings expose political interests within theories of media, but formal distinctions are not therefore meaningless.
full: The historical readings expose political interests within theories of media, but formal distinctions are not therefore meaningless. Painting and speech do possess materially different affordances. Showing that a distinction is ideological does not establish that it is entirely fabricated.

kernel: The dual definition of ideology preserves both critical force and reflexivity, but it also creates instability.
full: The dual definition of ideology preserves both critical force and reflexivity, but it also creates instability. Ideology sometimes means domination concealed as nature and sometimes any system of values informing representation. Under the second definition, everything is ideological; under the first, criteria are needed to distinguish domination from ordinary situatedness.

kernel: The critique of iconoclasm risks making all attacks on representation appear self-defeating.
full: The critique of iconoclasm risks making all attacks on representation appear self-defeating. Yet some images, media systems, and representational practices genuinely warrant destruction, refusal, censorship, redesign, or removal. Reflexivity alone does not decide which interventions are justified.

kernel: The canon is overwhelmingly Western, male, philosophical, and literary.
full: The canon is overwhelmingly Western, male, philosophical, and literary. The book brilliantly historicizes Western image anxiety while sometimes allowing that anxiety to stand in for the politics of imagery as such.

kernel: The article claims that the text-image distinction “collapses,” but the system depends on maintaining modality-specific encoders, decoders, losses, interfaces, datasets, and output formats.
full: The article claims that the text-image distinction “collapses,” but the system depends on maintaining modality-specific encoders, decoders, losses, interfaces, datasets, and output formats. Shared representation does not mean identical processing or identical material form.

kernel: The sequential-connectionist opposition is conceptually useful but technically too clean.
full: The sequential-connectionist opposition is conceptually useful but technically too clean. Neural networks are implemented through sequential code, accelerator kernels, preprocessing pipelines, tokenizers, schedulers, and deterministic control systems. Contemporary AI is a layered hybrid, not a replacement of one paradigm by another.

kernel: The claim that connectionist systems contain no “first text” understates the role of source code, architecture specifications, training instructions, labels, prompts, and configuration files.
full: The claim that connectionist systems contain no “first text” understates the role of source code, architecture specifications, training instructions, labels, prompts, and configuration files. Rules have not disappeared; many have moved from the output behavior into the construction of the learning system.

kernel: Treating an encoded image file as “text” conflates text with discrete symbolic data.
full: Treating an encoded image file as “text” conflates text with discrete symbolic data. Binary or numerical encoding does not automatically become textual merely because it can be represented alphanumerically. Under that definition, every digital object becomes text and the term loses discriminatory force.

kernel: The argument denies operative status to analogue visual poetry because bodily action mediates production.
full: The argument denies operative status to analogue visual poetry because bodily action mediates production. Yet computational output also depends on physical work: keystrokes, electricity, processors, storage, data centers, and human maintenance. Excluding analogue causality privileges informational operations over material ones.

kernel: Cross-modal correlation demonstrates that a model can align patterns associated with the same concept.
full: Cross-modal correlation demonstrates that a model can align patterns associated with the same concept. It does not establish semantics in the stronger senses of reference, communicative intention, social use, truth conditions, or practical understanding.

kernel: Grounding text in images may still leave both modalities ungrounded in the world.
full: Grounding text in images may still leave both modalities ungrounded in the world. A caption and a photograph can correlate while reproducing the same mistaken category, cultural stereotype, staged scene, or inherited bias.

kernel: The visual-poetry examples imply that technical substrate determines the ontology of the work.
full: The visual-poetry examples imply that technical substrate determines the ontology of the work. But reception also matters: viewers may encounter the final image without knowing whether it came from a typewriter, script, or neural model. Media specificity and perceptual experience do not always align.

kernel: The paper defines AGI through broad reasoning, planning, and learning from experience, then calls GPT-4 an early AGI while acknowledging that planning is substantially deficient and continuous learning is absent.
full: The paper defines AGI through broad reasoning, planning, and learning from experience, then calls GPT-4 an early AGI while acknowledging that planning is substantially deficient and continuous learning is absent. The conclusion depends on treating breadth as sufficient evidence despite missing parts of the stated definition.

kernel: The authors reject conventional benchmarks because training-data contamination is unknowable, but replace them with hand-selected demonstrations whose novelty, difficulty, and representativeness are also difficult to establish.
full: The authors reject conventional benchmarks because training-data contamination is unknowable, but replace them with hand-selected demonstrations whose novelty, difficulty, and representativeness are also difficult to establish.

kernel: Human creativity is presented as an evaluation strength, yet it introduces researcher discretion at every stage: which prompts are attempted, which outputs are shown, how often a task is retried, which comparison seems fair, and what counts as impressive.
full: Human creativity is presented as an evaluation strength, yet it introduces researcher discretion at every stage: which prompts are attempted, which outputs are shown, how often a task is retried, which comparison seems fair, and what counts as impressive.

kernel: The model was changing during the investigation.
full: The model was changing during the investigation. The improving TikZ unicorn is offered as evidence of development, but a moving target prevents clean reproduction, stable measurement, and attribution of changes to particular interventions.

kernel: The paper sometimes treats successful output as evidence of internal understanding while explaining failures through architectural constraints, prompting, or evaluation design.
full: The paper sometimes treats successful output as evidence of internal understanding while explaining failures through architectural constraints, prompting, or evaluation design. Success is readily attributed to the model; failure is more often distributed across the surrounding system.

kernel: GPT-4 is occasionally asked to evaluate its own work against another model’s output.
full: GPT-4 is occasionally asked to evaluate its own work against another model’s output. This introduces circularity, possible self-preference, and dependence on the same model whose judgment is under investigation.

kernel: The paper calls generated visual code evidence that a text-only system can “see.” More precisely, it demonstrates that the model can manipulate textual representations whose execution produces recognizable geometry.
full: The paper calls generated visual code evidence that a text-only system can “see.” More precisely, it demonstrates that the model can manipulate textual representations whose execution produces recognizable geometry. That is significant, but it is not equivalent to perceptual contact with images.

kernel: The claim of near-human performance compares a persistent, rapidly sampled model against loosely specified humans.
full: The claim of near-human performance compares a persistent, rapidly sampled model against loosely specified humans. Human expertise, preparation, time, access to tools, incentives, and evaluation conditions are not consistently matched.

kernel: Geertz rejects culture as a causal force and defines it as context, yet contexts influence what actions are possible, intelligible, rewarded, or punished.
full: Geertz rejects culture as a causal force and defines it as context, yet contexts influence what actions are possible, intelligible, rewarded, or punished. The distinction between explanation by meaning and explanation by cause is less clean than the argument suggests.

kernel: Meaning is declared public, but intention still matters centrally to distinguishing wink, parody, rehearsal, and fraud.
full: Meaning is declared public, but intention still matters centrally to distinguishing wink, parody, rehearsal, and fraud. The account resists mentalism while repeatedly relying on inferred purposes that cannot be directly observed.

kernel: The ethnographer is warned against pretending to become native, yet must formulate accounts in terms of actors’ own interpretations.
full: The ethnographer is warned against pretending to become native, yet must formulate accounts in terms of actors’ own interpretations. The method requires proximity to another point of view while denying that full access is possible.

kernel: Geertz emphasizes the fashioned, fictional character of ethnographic writing but defends its claim to knowledge through interpretive power.
full: Geertz emphasizes the fashioned, fictional character of ethnographic writing but defends its claim to knowledge through interpretive power. The criteria separating disciplined construction from persuasive invention remain under-specified.

kernel: The article criticizes formalism and the dream of an “ethnographic algorithm,” yet it offers reusable conceptual structures-thin versus thick description, inscription, public meaning, symbolic action-that can themselves harden into a new formula.
full: The article criticizes formalism and the dream of an “ethnographic algorithm,” yet it offers reusable conceptual structures-thin versus thick description, inscription, public meaning, symbolic action-that can themselves harden into a new formula.

kernel: Interpretation must stay close to specific events, but it is also expected to speak to large questions.
full: Interpretation must stay close to specific events, but it is also expected to speak to large questions. “Small facts speak to large issues because they are made to” accurately names the inferential leap without fully regulating it.

kernel: Theory is said to generalize within cases rather than across them, yet concepts such as culture, symbol, discourse, and meaning are repeatedly moved between very different cases.
full: Theory is said to generalize within cases rather than across them, yet concepts such as culture, symbol, discourse, and meaning are repeatedly moved between very different cases. The distinction protects particularity but cannot eliminate cross-case abstraction.

kernel: Geertz treats actor-oriented interpretation as essential, but actors themselves disagree, deceive, misunderstand, and inhabit unequal positions.
full: Geertz treats actor-oriented interpretation as essential, but actors themselves disagree, deceive, misunderstand, and inhabit unequal positions. There may be no singular “actor’s point of view” to recover.

kernel: The account treats public meaning as accessible through interpretation while also insisting that analysis is intrinsically incomplete.
full: The account treats public meaning as accessible through interpretation while also insisting that analysis is intrinsically incomplete. This leaves appraisal dependent partly on judgment, disciplinary authority, and rhetorical force.

kernel: A simulator normally supports interventions: an agent takes an action and the environment returns a consequential next state.
full: A simulator normally supports interventions: an agent takes an action and the environment returns a consequential next state. Most evidence presented here concerns unconditional or prompt-conditioned video generation, not systematic action-conditioned dynamics.

kernel: Visual plausibility is not causal fidelity.
full: Visual plausibility is not causal fidelity. A video may look physically coherent while relying on incorrect hidden mechanics. Conversely, a physically accurate simulation may look visually crude.

kernel: The report cites object permanence and state persistence as emergent capabilities while acknowledging spontaneous object appearances, incorrect eating behavior, broken physics, and long-duration incoherence.
full: The report cites object permanence and state persistence as emergent capabilities while acknowledging spontaneous object appearances, incorrect eating behavior, broken physics, and long-duration incoherence. The evidence for simulation and the evidence against it arise from the same outputs.

kernel: Interpolation between two videos can produce smooth transitions without representing any possible path through the world.
full: Interpolation between two videos can produce smooth transitions without representing any possible path through the world. Semantic morphing may resemble temporal continuity while violating identity, causality, or material conservation.

kernel: The Minecraft example is framed as simulation of a digital world, but visual imitation of gameplay does not establish that the model has learned the game’s rule system, inventory logic, collision mechanics, or persistent hidden state.
full: The Minecraft example is framed as simulation of a digital world, but visual imitation of gameplay does not establish that the model has learned the game’s rule system, inventory logic, collision mechanics, or persistent hidden state.

kernel: The claim that 3D consistency emerges without explicit 3D inductive bias is important, but training video already contains structured projections of three-dimensional scenes.
full: The claim that 3D consistency emerges without explicit 3D inductive bias is important, but training video already contains structured projections of three-dimensional scenes. The bias may be present statistically in the data rather than architecturally in the model.

kernel: Scaling evidence is presented qualitatively through selected outputs at different compute levels.
full: Scaling evidence is presented qualitatively through selected outputs at different compute levels. Improved sample quality does not by itself establish improved causal modeling, physical accuracy, or general simulation competence.

kernel: The report calls Sora a generalist model while withholding model architecture details, training data composition, evaluation procedures, and implementation specifics.
full: The report calls Sora a generalist model while withholding model architecture details, training data composition, evaluation procedures, and implementation specifics. Generality is asserted through demonstrations that outside researchers cannot fully reproduce.

kernel: Prompt expansion improves adherence but complicates attribution.
full: Prompt expansion improves adherence but complicates attribution. It becomes unclear whether a successful output reflects the video model’s understanding, the captioning model’s elaboration, hidden prompting conventions, or selective sampling.

kernel: The page defines world models as systems that “understand” physical dynamics, but most described capabilities concern generation, prediction, curation, and post-training.
full: The page defines world models as systems that “understand” physical dynamics, but most described capabilities concern generation, prediction, curation, and post-training. Successful visual continuation does not establish understanding in a strong causal sense.

kernel: Traditional rule-based simulators are described as precise but brittle, while learned models are presented as adaptive and general.
full: Traditional rule-based simulators are described as precise but brittle, while learned models are presented as adaptive and general. Yet learned systems can be less interpretable, less constrained, and less reliable precisely in the rare conditions where physical systems require guarantees.

kernel: The claim that autoregressive models “understand cause and effect in time” is stronger than the architecture alone supports.
full: The claim that autoregressive models “understand cause and effect in time” is stronger than the architecture alone supports. Sequential prediction can learn statistical regularities without identifying causal mechanisms.

kernel: The page says diffusion models refine outputs simultaneously and provide stronger spatial consistency, but diffusion still proceeds through multiple denoising steps.
full: The page says diffusion models refine outputs simultaneously and provide stronger spatial consistency, but diffusion still proceeds through multiple denoising steps. “Simultaneous” describes position-wise generation within a pass more than the absence of temporal computation.

kernel: Synthetic data are presented as enabling performance on unseen situations.
full: Synthetic data are presented as enabling performance on unseen situations. But synthetic environments can only vary along dimensions represented by their generators, simulators, prompts, and scenario designers. Unknown unknowns cannot be reliably generated by a model that does not represent them.

kernel: Photorealism is presented as helping close the sim-to-real gap.
full: Photorealism is presented as helping close the sim-to-real gap. Visual resemblance can reduce perceptual mismatch while leaving friction, deformability, latency, sensor noise, human unpredictability, and hidden physical state incorrectly modeled.

kernel: The claim that one foundation model can generalize across embodiments underplays how strongly action possibilities depend on body geometry, sensors, actuators, control frequency, balance, energy, and failure tolerance.
full: The claim that one foundation model can generalize across embodiments underplays how strongly action possibilities depend on body geometry, sensors, actuators, control frequency, balance, energy, and failure tolerance.

kernel: The page describes reinforcement learning as giving models the ability to “reason for themselves.” RL optimizes behavior against rewards; it does not by itself establish reasoning, self-understanding, or reliable internal deliberation.
full: The page describes reinforcement learning as giving models the ability to “reason for themselves.” RL optimizes behavior against rewards; it does not by itself establish reasoning, self-understanding, or reliable internal deliberation.

kernel: The system is promoted as accelerating development from years to months, but the hidden labor of data acquisition, annotation, simulator calibration, safety evaluation, hardware integration, and domain validation remains substantial.
full: The system is promoted as accelerating development from years to months, but the hidden labor of data acquisition, annotation, simulator calibration, safety evaluation, hardware integration, and domain validation remains substantial.

kernel: DeepMind defines a world model as predicting how an environment evolves and how actions affect it, but the demonstrated action space is mostly navigation.
full: DeepMind defines a world model as predicting how an environment evolves and how actions affect it, but the demonstrated action space is mostly navigation. Locomotion through generated scenery is a weaker test than manipulating objects, operating tools, or causing irreversible physical changes.

kernel: Promptable world events are described as counterfactual interventions, yet they are often externally imposed scene edits rather than consequences of agent action.
full: Promptable world events are described as counterfactual interventions, yet they are often externally imposed scene edits rather than consequences of agent action. Changing the weather through a prompt does not test whether the model understands how an agent could cause or respond to that change.

kernel: The system is praised for consistency lasting several minutes, while one minute of visual memory and a few minutes of interaction remain extremely short relative to real tasks, institutions, journeys, or embodied learning.
full: The system is praised for consistency lasting several minutes, while one minute of visual memory and a few minutes of interaction remain extremely short relative to real tasks, institutions, journeys, or embodied learning.

kernel: Frame-by-frame generation allows rich dynamic environments but also creates compounding error.
full: Frame-by-frame generation allows rich dynamic environments but also creates compounding error. The same autoregressive process that supports interactivity can gradually rewrite geometry, identity, and causal history.

kernel: The comparison with NeRFs and Gaussian splatting emphasizes dynamism, but explicit 3D representations offer inspectable geometry and stronger viewpoint consistency.
full: The comparison with NeRFs and Gaussian splatting emphasizes dynamism, but explicit 3D representations offer inspectable geometry and stronger viewpoint consistency. Genie trades structural certainty for generative flexibility.

kernel: The model is presented as useful for embodied-agent training, yet its environments may lack stable affordances, collision rules, object properties, and reproducible transitions.
full: The model is presented as useful for embodied-agent training, yet its environments may lack stable affordances, collision rules, object properties, and reproducible transitions. An agent could learn to navigate visual regularities that do not correspond to any dependable mechanics.

kernel: The natural-world examples demonstrate striking appearance, not validated ecology or physics.
full: The natural-world examples demonstrate striking appearance, not validated ecology or physics. A convincing hurricane, jellyfish swarm, or volcanic terrain does not establish accurate fluid dynamics, animal behavior, material risk, or sensor response.

kernel: The system can simulate recognizable locations and historical settings while admitting geographic inaccuracy.
full: The system can simulate recognizable locations and historical settings while admitting geographic inaccuracy. This creates a tension between experiential vividness and epistemic reliability, especially for education.

kernel: The release frames limited access as responsible development, but restricted access also prevents independent auditing of consistency, memorization, dataset provenance, failure rates, and safety claims.
full: The release frames limited access as responsible development, but restricted access also prevents independent auditing of consistency, memorization, dataset provenance, failure rates, and safety claims.

kernel: The survey defines world models functionally through downstream usefulness.
full: The survey defines world models functionally through downstream usefulness. This avoids equating video generation with simulation, but risks circularity: a predictive component may be called a world model whenever it improves a policy, even if it contains little interpretable environmental structure.

kernel: Low-level motor commands and high-level language instructions are both treated as “actions.” Yet a motor command intervenes physically, while an instruction specifies a desired outcome.
full: Low-level motor commands and high-level language instructions are both treated as “actions.” Yet a motor command intervenes physically, while an instruction specifies a desired outcome. Conflating the two can blur the distinction between causally producing a future and merely describing one.

kernel: Tighter integration promises stronger performance but weaker diagnosis.
full: Tighter integration promises stronger performance but weaker diagnosis. In a decoupled pipeline, researchers can inspect the imagined future and the inverse controller separately. In a unified backbone, prediction and action errors may be impossible to disentangle.

kernel: Latent world models avoid the cost and redundancy of pixels, but their internal predictions become harder to audit.
full: Latent world models avoid the cost and redundancy of pixels, but their internal predictions become harder to audit. A representation may improve control while encoding shortcuts, spurious correlations, or embodiment-specific assumptions invisible to the evaluator.

kernel: A simulator used to train and score a policy can reward behavior that exploits simulator errors.
full: A simulator used to train and score a policy can reward behavior that exploits simulator errors. High imagined return may indicate genuine competence-or successful manipulation of the model’s blind spots.

kernel: Co-evolution can repair simulator weaknesses, but it can also create a closed epistemic circuit.
full: Co-evolution can repair simulator weaknesses, but it can also create a closed epistemic circuit. If real-world testing is sparse, the policy and world model may become mutually consistent while jointly drifting away from physical reality.

kernel: The literature emphasizes video because visual data scale readily, yet manipulation depends heavily on force, touch, proprioception, compliance, weight, friction, and damage.
full: The literature emphasizes video because visual data scale readily, yet manipulation depends heavily on force, touch, proprioception, compliance, weight, friction, and damage. Camera-visible futures are only one slice of embodied state.

kernel: Benchmark gains often compare complete systems with different backbones, data, policy heads, inference budgets, and training recipes.
full: Benchmark gains often compare complete systems with different backbones, data, policy heads, inference budgets, and training recipes. It can therefore remain unclear whether improvement comes from world modeling specifically or from a larger surrounding stack.

kernel: The survey describes a trend toward foundation-scale generality while acknowledging that cross-embodiment transfer remains dependent on specific action spaces and bodies.
full: The survey describes a trend toward foundation-scale generality while acknowledging that cross-embodiment transfer remains dependent on specific action spaces and bodies. A shared visual prior does not imply a shared capacity to act.

kernel: The paper calls WorldDreamer a “general world model,” but action conditioning is demonstrated principally in the narrow domain of autonomous driving, using yaw and velocity.
full: The paper calls WorldDreamer a “general world model,” but action conditioning is demonstrated principally in the narrow domain of autonomous driving, using yaw and velocity. Most other tasks are passive generation or editing rather than action-conditioned simulation.

kernel: The claim that the model learns “general world physics and motions” is supported mainly through visual examples.
full: The claim that the model learns “general world physics and motions” is supported mainly through visual examples. Smooth video, consistent framing, and plausible movement do not establish correct mass, force, collision, conservation, or causal intervention.

kernel: Masked-token reconstruction can exploit information from surrounding frames unavailable in real-time prediction.
full: Masked-token reconstruction can exploit information from surrounding frames unavailable in real-time prediction. A model that completes a missing middle frame using both past and future context may perform well without possessing the forward dynamics needed by an acting agent.

kernel: The paper invokes a Markovian assumption to justify predicting multiple tokens in parallel.
full: The paper invokes a Markovian assumption to justify predicting multiple tokens in parallel. But many visually important events depend on long-range hidden state: prior contact, object ownership, off-screen motion, accumulated damage, or an earlier decision.

kernel: The same token mask is applied across frames to prevent leakage from adjacent visual similarity.
full: The same token mask is applied across frames to prevent leakage from adjacent visual similarity. This reduces one shortcut but does not eliminate others, including static backgrounds, camera regularities, dataset conventions, and caption-derived priors.

kernel: Training simultaneously on videos and sets of independent images increases data volume, but the image batches contain no genuine temporal relation.
full: Training simultaneously on videos and sets of independent images increases data volume, but the image batches contain no genuine temporal relation. The model must learn spatial statistics from one source and temporal dynamics from another without a clear guarantee that the two combine into coherent physical knowledge.

kernel: Detailed captions are generated by Gemini, meaning the visual-language relationship is partially authored by another model.
full: Detailed captions are generated by Gemini, meaning the visual-language relationship is partially authored by another model. WorldDreamer may learn Gemini’s descriptive ontology and omissions as much as the underlying contents of the videos.

kernel: The self-collected dataset filters out clips with slow optical flow.
full: The self-collected dataset filters out clips with slow optical flow. This favors visibly dynamic footage and may underrepresent stillness, subtle contact, gradual change, and causally important events that produce little pixel motion.

kernel: Parallel decoding provides major speed advantages, but committing high-confidence tokens early can lock in a globally wrong interpretation.
full: Parallel decoding provides major speed advantages, but committing high-confidence tokens early can lock in a globally wrong interpretation. Confidence in local appearance is not equivalent to consistency of the complete event.

kernel: The paper presents masked generation as a route to general world modeling, yet its outputs are only 24 frames at 192×320 resolution.
full: The paper presents masked generation as a route to general world modeling, yet its outputs are only 24 frames at 192×320 resolution. The temporal and spatial horizon remains far below the scale needed for persistent interactive environments.

kernel: A video model may know what manipulation should look like while failing to preserve the actual robot.
full: A video model may know what manipulation should look like while failing to preserve the actual robot. The Veo example produces plausible motion but changes the gripper, kinematics, and arm morphology. Visual knowledge of an action category is not knowledge of this embodiment’s action possibilities.

kernel: The argument that inverse dynamics is easier assumes the predicted future is correct and physically reachable.
full: The argument that inverse dynamics is easier assumes the predicted future is correct and physically reachable. An elegant imagined goal can make action inference simpler while simultaneously directing the robot toward an impossible state.

kernel: Video quality and policy quality may correlate because both improve with scale, data curation, and stronger backbones.
full: Video quality and policy quality may correlate because both improve with scale, data curation, and stronger backbones. That correlation does not isolate future prediction as the causal source of better control.

kernel: Representation-only systems challenge the term “world-action model.” If future generation is skipped at inference and only pretrained features remain, the architecture may function more like a temporally informed policy than an active world model.
full: Representation-only systems challenge the term “world-action model.” If future generation is skipped at inference and only pretrained features remain, the architecture may function more like a temporally informed policy than an active world model.

kernel: Action-as-image avoids modality mismatch but introduces semantic mismatch.
full: Action-as-image avoids modality mismatch but introduces semantic mismatch. Spatially averaging a synthetic frame into an action vector is computationally convenient, yet no physical reason requires motor control to possess image topology.

kernel: WAMs promise better physical grounding while relying overwhelmingly on camera-visible evidence.
full: WAMs promise better physical grounding while relying overwhelmingly on camera-visible evidence. Force, torque, friction, compliance, temperature, weight, pain, and mechanical damage may be weakly inferable or invisible in ordinary video.

kernel: The article presents WAMs as a second major recipe, but the strongest projected endpoint is a hybrid that combines WAM and VLA components.
full: The article presents WAMs as a second major recipe, but the strongest projected endpoint is a hybrid that combines WAM and VLA components. Success may dissolve the category whose rise the article announces.

kernel: Reported comparisons mix different models, training corpora, parameter counts, action representations, inference budgets, embodiments, and evaluation environments.
full: Reported comparisons mix different models, training corpora, parameter counts, action representations, inference budgets, embodiments, and evaluation environments. A leaderboard advantage cannot by itself identify the winning representation paradigm.

kernel: Open video backbones democratize WAM research downstream while concentrating upstream power.
full: Open video backbones democratize WAM research downstream while concentrating upstream power. Robotics laboratories can fine-tune the models, but only a small number of organizations can determine their datasets, tokenizers, physical priors, and representational omissions.

kernel: The language gives the programmer precise control over images while requiring thought at the level of memory locations, scanner positions, raster coordinates, and modulo arithmetic.
full: The language gives the programmer precise control over images while requiring thought at the level of memory locations, scanner positions, raster coordinates, and modulo arithmetic. Expressive power is purchased through cognitive burden.

kernel: The system describes scanners as intuitive conceptual devices, yet their behavior is constrained by machine-specific storage topology.
full: The system describes scanners as intuitive conceptual devices, yet their behavior is constrained by machine-specific storage topology. Moving beyond a surface edge may wrap helically, enter unused memory, or produce unexpected coordinates. The imagined visual space and the actual computational space do not fully coincide.

kernel: The movie language abstracts animation into high-level operations, but many operations remain tied to integer grids and rectangular regions.
full: The movie language abstracts animation into high-level operations, but many operations remain tied to integer grids and rectangular regions. Curves, zooms, rotations, and scaling are approximations produced through deletion, repetition, or local stepping.

kernel: The system allows several scanners to occupy the same square, but their internal memories may disagree after one modifies the value.
full: The system allows several scanners to occupy the same square, but their internal memories may disagree after one modifies the value. Multiple agents can share a location while possessing inconsistent beliefs about its state.

kernel: The camera’s grey-scale image is produced through character glyphs and deliberate optical defocus.
full: The camera’s grey-scale image is produced through character glyphs and deliberate optical defocus. Apparent continuous tone depends upon exploiting an output device designed for text rather than directly controlling luminance.

kernel: The system compresses repeated frames efficiently, but final temporal realization still depends on optical printing and laboratory work.
full: The system compresses repeated frames efficiently, but final temporal realization still depends on optical printing and laboratory work. Digital specification does not yet eliminate the analog production chain.

kernel: Dynamic transformations such as dissolve and zoom are predefined algorithms rather than continuously parameterized spatial models.
full: Dynamic transformations such as dissolve and zoom are predefined algorithms rather than continuously parameterized spatial models. Their cinematic appearance is flexible within narrow procedural templates.

kernel: The language is extensible through macros, but this extensibility can also fragment practice.
full: The language is extensible through macros, but this extensibility can also fragment practice. Each programmer may create a private vocabulary whose semantics are legible only within a particular project.

kernel: The chapter describes computers as protection from human ignorance, but this transfers judgment to whoever designs the system.
full: The chapter describes computers as protection from human ignorance, but this transfers judgment to whoever designs the system. A machine cannot make a person “fail-safe” without embedding somebody’s definition of failure, acceptable conduct, and desirable life.

kernel: The language of symbiosis suggests ecological reciprocity while obscuring asymmetry.
full: The language of symbiosis suggests ecological reciprocity while obscuring asymmetry. Institutions own the hardware, define access, write the software, select permissible parameters, and decide which outputs circulate.

kernel: The computer is presented as liberating humanity from specialization, yet the practice described requires unusual concentrations of mathematical, engineering, artistic, and institutional expertise.
full: The computer is presented as liberating humanity from specialization, yet the practice described requires unusual concentrations of mathematical, engineering, artistic, and institutional expertise. Liberation from one specialization produces dependence on an entire technical organization.

kernel: The chapter celebrates the computer as an active collaborator while repeatedly asserting that it performs only prescribed operations.
full: The chapter celebrates the computer as an active collaborator while repeatedly asserting that it performs only prescribed operations. “Unexpected” output may arise from combinatorial complexity, but surprise to the artist does not itself establish autonomous judgment.

kernel: Mallary’s six levels treat removal of the human artist as the apex of cybernetic development.
full: Mallary’s six levels treat removal of the human artist as the apex of cybernetic development. This confuses increasing machine autonomy with increasing aesthetic value. A system’s independence does not guarantee sensitivity, accountability, or significance.

kernel: The proposed psychedelic computer can optimize or steer emotion, but the text treats this mainly as expanded consciousness.
full: The proposed psychedelic computer can optimize or steer emotion, but the text treats this mainly as expanded consciousness. The same apparatus is equally a system for behavioral conditioning, persuasion, surveillance, and affective capture.

kernel: The new artist is imagined as one individual fluent in both art and technology.
full: The new artist is imagined as one individual fluent in both art and technology. This resolves communication problems by idealizing the hybrid genius while backgrounding collaboration, maintenance, institutional support, technicians, programmers, processors, and operators.

kernel: Youngblood criticizes simplistic computer art whose importance derives only from its technological origin, yet his descriptions sometimes grant technological novelty an almost automatic transcendental force.
full: Youngblood criticizes simplistic computer art whose importance derives only from its technological origin, yet his descriptions sometimes grant technological novelty an almost automatic transcendental force.

kernel: The chapter’s utopian abundance assumes materials become interchangeable and knowledge becomes the unique resource.
full: The chapter’s utopian abundance assumes materials become interchangeable and knowledge becomes the unique resource. Physical extraction, energy, manufacturing, labor, waste, infrastructure, and unequal access are rhetorically dissolved into information.

kernel: Whitney borrows music as the model for visual time while repeatedly warning against simplistic equivalence between sound and image.
full: Whitney borrows music as the model for visual time while repeatedly warning against simplistic equivalence between sound and image. His theory depends on analogy but succeeds only where the analogy is not treated literally.

kernel: Whole-number ratios produce clear periodic resonances, but perceptual value cannot be reduced to mathematical consonance.
full: Whole-number ratios produce clear periodic resonances, but perceptual value cannot be reduced to mathematical consonance. A visually exact resonance may be aesthetically dull, while irregular motion may be highly structured and emotionally powerful.

kernel: He rejects randomness as aimless motion, yet unexpected variation can create tension, rupture, and renewal.
full: He rejects randomness as aimless motion, yet unexpected variation can create tension, rupture, and renewal. The real opposition is not order versus randomness, but consequential variation versus unstructured noise.

kernel: The theory treats resolution as emotionally central, but some temporal works deliberately sustain ambiguity, suspension, or irreconcilable conflict.
full: The theory treats resolution as emotionally central, but some temporal works deliberately sustain ambiguity, suspension, or irreconcilable conflict. Not every meaningful tension seeks consonant release.

kernel: Whitney criticizes abstraction because it draws attention toward the objects rather than the dynamics, but his preferred geometric forms are conventionally classified as abstract.
full: Whitney criticizes abstraction because it draws attention toward the objects rather than the dynamics, but his preferred geometric forms are conventionally classified as abstract. He rejects less the nonrepresentational image than an object-centered account of it.

kernel: The search for innate visual harmony risks universalizing historically and culturally situated aesthetic preferences.
full: The search for innate visual harmony risks universalizing historically and culturally situated aesthetic preferences. Shared sensitivity to periodicity does not establish one universal grammar of visual meaning.

kernel: His field model activates all elements continuously, yet stillness can be a powerful temporal operation.
full: His field model activates all elements continuously, yet stillness can be a powerful temporal operation. Absence, pause, withholding, and near-immobility may structure expectation as strongly as overt differential motion.

kernel: The comparison with music privileges organized temporal continuity and can marginalize cinematic operations such as discontinuity, montage, interruption, narrative ellipsis, and incompatible temporal registers.
full: The comparison with music privileges organized temporal continuity and can marginalize cinematic operations such as discontinuity, montage, interruption, narrative ellipsis, and incompatible temporal registers.

kernel: The theory describes viewer engagement as a dialogue with the composer, but the actual work is fixed before reception.
full: The theory describes viewer engagement as a dialogue with the composer, but the actual work is fixed before reception. The viewer predicts and interprets, yet cannot alter the temporal system’s progression.

kernel: The metaphor of culture as a “program” clarifies how symbols guide action, but risks making people appear as machines executing inherited instructions.
full: The metaphor of culture as a “program” clarifies how symbols guide action, but risks making people appear as machines executing inherited instructions. Actual cultural life includes improvisation, conflict, refusal, ambiguity, error, and deliberate rewriting of the program.

kernel: Geertz attacks the separation of biological, psychological, social, and cultural levels, yet his own account sometimes distinguishes innate capacities from cultural realization too neatly.
full: Geertz attacks the separation of biological, psychological, social, and cultural levels, yet his own account sometimes distinguishes innate capacities from cultural realization too neatly. The exact boundary remains, as he admits, unstable and interactive.

kernel: The essay rejects empty universals but still needs general claims about humans: plasticity, incompleteness, symbolic dependence, and cultural-biological coevolution.
full: The essay rejects empty universals but still needs general claims about humans: plasticity, incompleteness, symbolic dependence, and cultural-biological coevolution. It does not abandon universality; it moves universality from content to developmental mechanism.

kernel: Culture is presented as necessary for ordering behavior, but cultural systems can also produce disorientation, contradiction, domination, and trauma.
full: Culture is presented as necessary for ordering behavior, but cultural systems can also produce disorientation, contradiction, domination, and trauma. Symbolic order is not necessarily coherent, benevolent, or collectively shared.

kernel: The emphasis on becoming human through particular culture can slide toward cultural enclosure.
full: The emphasis on becoming human through particular culture can slide toward cultural enclosure. Individuals inhabit multiple, overlapping, mobile, and conflicting symbolic systems rather than one bounded culture that completes them uniformly.

kernel: Geertz treats particularity as the route to human truth, yet interpretation of particularity still depends on theoretical abstraction.
full: Geertz treats particularity as the route to human truth, yet interpretation of particularity still depends on theoretical abstraction. Detail does not explain itself; the anthropologist decides which differences are consequential.

kernel: The positive-feedback account of biological and cultural development is powerful but can become teleological.
full: The positive-feedback account of biological and cultural development is powerful but can become teleological. It may appear that cultural evolution was directing humanity toward its present condition rather than producing contingent, branching, and sometimes extinct possibilities.

kernel: Culture supplies the symbolic means by which emotions are formed and recognized, but affect also exceeds explicit cultural coding through bodily states, developmental histories, and situations that resist available names.
full: Culture supplies the symbolic means by which emotions are formed and recognized, but affect also exceeds explicit cultural coding through bodily states, developmental histories, and situations that resist available names.

kernel: The claim that every person is a cultural artifact foregrounds formation but can understate power.
full: The claim that every person is a cultural artifact foregrounds formation but can understate power. People are not only shaped by shared meanings; they are governed by unequal institutions controlling land, education, violence, records, and access to representation.

kernel: Sterrett’s historical case is cumulative but circumstantial.
full: Sterrett’s historical case is cumulative but circumstantial. Wittgenstein’s engineering interests, books, travel, and 1914 milieu make dimensional analysis a plausible influence, but they do not prove that it supplied the architecture of the Tractatus.

kernel: The analogy between Tractarian objects and physical dimensions is illuminating but imperfect.
full: The analogy between Tractarian objects and physical dimensions is illuminating but imperfect. Dimensions are abstract kinds of measurable quantity; Wittgenstein’s objects are supposed to be simple constituents of possible worlds.

kernel: Physical similarity explains engineered quantitative models especially well.
full: Physical similarity explains engineered quantitative models especially well. It is less obvious how the same account handles ordinary propositions about pain, promises, color, politics, jokes, or intention.

kernel: Intertranslatability requires machines, conventions, and trained users.
full: Intertranslatability requires machines, conventions, and trained users. If logical form depends upon those practices, it begins to resemble a socially maintained technique rather than an intrinsic structure shared by representations independently of their users.

kernel: A physically similar model preserves only selected behaviors.
full: A physically similar model preserves only selected behaviors. Yet the Tractatus often appears to seek a general account of representation. The analogy may replace one universal form with many purpose-relative similarity relations.

kernel: The courtroom model mixes two different orders: physical causality and normative responsibility.
full: The courtroom model mixes two different orders: physical causality and normative responsibility. A miniature can demonstrate that one trajectory produces a collision, but it cannot by physical similarity alone establish who was legally or morally at fault.

kernel: The parallel between removing arithmetic constants from general physical equations and removing logical constants from propositions may be structurally suggestive without being philosophically identical.
full: The parallel between removing arithmetic constants from general physical equations and removing logical constants from propositions may be structurally suggestive without being philosophically identical.

kernel: Sterrett emphasizes measurement systems as securing contact with reality, but systems of measurement are themselves historically designed, standardized, and institutionally enforced.
full: Sterrett emphasizes measurement systems as securing contact with reality, but systems of measurement are themselves historically designed, standardized, and institutionally enforced. The measure is not external to culture.

kernel: Geertz defines symbols broadly enough to include numbers and computer programs, yet his strongest examples come from religion and art.
full: Geertz defines symbols broadly enough to include numbers and computer programs, yet his strongest examples come from religion and art. The account leaves underdeveloped how scientific equations, instruments, standards, and models acquire meaning differently from ritual symbols.

kernel: The distinction between models of and models for reality is analytically useful, but most symbolic systems perform both functions.
full: The distinction between models of and models for reality is analytically useful, but most symbolic systems perform both functions. A scientific climate model describes possible futures while shaping policy; a poem represents experience while teaching perception.

kernel: Religion is described as clothing conceptions with factuality, but the same process occurs in law, economics, nationalism, medicine, and algorithmic classification.
full: Religion is described as clothing conceptions with factuality, but the same process occurs in law, economics, nationalism, medicine, and algorithmic classification. The mechanism may be broader than religion.

kernel: Geertz emphasizes public symbolic systems while moods are partly embodied, pre-reflective, and resistant to articulation.
full: Geertz emphasizes public symbolic systems while moods are partly embodied, pre-reflective, and resistant to articulation. Cultural symbols can organize affect without fully creating or controlling it.

kernel: The account suggests that ritual validates worldview through experienced coherence.
full: The account suggests that ritual validates worldview through experienced coherence. But internally coherent ritual can sustain false, oppressive, or self-sealing systems that reinterpret all counterevidence as confirmation.

kernel: Religion responds to the threat of chaos by affirming interpretability.
full: Religion responds to the threat of chaos by affirming interpretability. Yet some religious traditions cultivate mystery, contradiction, silence, or the limits of human comprehension rather than a fully intelligible order.

kernel: Geertz contrasts religion with science through commitment versus doubt, but scientific communities also depend on trust, inherited authority, disciplinary ritual, and taken-for-granted instruments.
full: Geertz contrasts religion with science through commitment versus doubt, but scientific communities also depend on trust, inherited authority, disciplinary ritual, and taken-for-granted instruments. Religious traditions can also contain inquiry, skepticism, and interpretive dispute.

kernel: The essay treats cultural patterns as programs guiding behavior, but people can misunderstand, combine, parody, reject, or strategically perform those programs.
full: The essay treats cultural patterns as programs guiding behavior, but people can misunderstand, combine, parody, reject, or strategically perform those programs. Symbolic direction is never equivalent to deterministic execution.

kernel: The transition from symbol to disposition remains difficult to specify.
full: The transition from symbol to disposition remains difficult to specify. The essay explains the relation interpretively but does not establish a causal mechanism showing how one ritual produces a durable motivation or mood in one person and not another.

kernel: The paper calls the models “implicit world models,” but the experiments do not clearly distinguish an internal model of latent state from a highly accurate conditional imitation of environment response strings.
full: The paper calls the models “implicit world models,” but the experiments do not clearly distinguish an internal model of latent state from a highly accurate conditional imitation of environment response strings.

kernel: Exact-match accuracy rewards literal reproduction of textual outputs.
full: Exact-match accuracy rewards literal reproduction of textual outputs. It can undercount semantically correct alternatives while overvaluing environments whose responses are templated and deterministic.

kernel: The paper argues that the models maintain hidden state, yet fuller initial configurations are supplied in some environments.
full: The paper argues that the models maintain hidden state, yet fuller initial configurations are supplied in some environments. State tracking and privileged initialization contribute jointly to performance but are not fully separated.

kernel: High consistency with strong agents partly reflects behavioral similarity to the GPT-4o policy used to collect training data.
full: High consistency with strong agents partly reflects behavioral similarity to the GPT-4o policy used to collect training data. The simulator appears most faithful when the acting policy stays near the behavior of its original demonstrator.

kernel: The W2R consistency ratio is a property of a simulator-policy pair, not solely of the simulator.
full: The W2R consistency ratio is a property of a simulator-policy pair, not solely of the simulator. A high ratio may result from conservative actions, easy tasks, agent adaptation, or model fidelity.

kernel: A consistency ratio greater than one does not mean the simulated world is “more correct” than reality.
full: A consistency ratio greater than one does not mean the simulated world is “more correct” than reality. It means that actions produced during simulation happened to transfer more successfully than the agent’s original real-environment behavior.

kernel: The synthetic-data experiment treats trajectories as successful according to the world model’s own predicted outcome.
full: The synthetic-data experiment treats trajectories as successful according to the world model’s own predicted outcome. The same simulator therefore generates the experience and initially certifies its value, creating a potential self-confirmation loop.

kernel: The early-experience experiment adds an additional supervised stage before policy training.
full: The early-experience experiment adds an additional supervised stage before policy training. Without matched controls for equivalent extra data and optimization, some gains may come from additional training rather than specifically from learning environmental dynamics.

kernel: Mixed-environment training improves several tasks, but this does not yet establish a domain-general world model.
full: Mixed-environment training improves several tasks, but this does not yet establish a domain-general world model. The gains may arise from regularization, shared response conventions, or reusable linguistic schemas.

kernel: Text environments reduce world interaction to discrete descriptions and commands.
full: Text environments reduce world interaction to discrete descriptions and commands. Success here does not establish competence with continuous state, noisy sensing, stochastic dynamics, force, embodiment, or irreversible physical consequence.

kernel: The model returns one textual future and a binary reward.
full: The model returns one textual future and a binary reward. It does not naturally expose multiple plausible outcomes, calibrated uncertainty, hidden causal variables, or probability over transitions.

kernel: If meaning consists exclusively in conceivable consequences, two conceptions with identical observable consequences become indistinguishable.
full: If meaning consists exclusively in conceivable consequences, two conceptions with identical observable consequences become indistinguishable. This can erase differences in explanation, ontology, historical origin, and moral framing that may matter even when immediate predictions coincide.

kernel: Peirce’s appeal to conduct under every possible situation seeks universal meaning, but no investigator can enumerate every conceivable experimental consequence.
full: Peirce’s appeal to conduct under every possible situation seeks universal meaning, but no investigator can enumerate every conceivable experimental consequence. The pragmatic definition may itself remain an ideal rather than an executable procedure.

kernel: James makes philosophical belief answerable to concrete experience, yet his existential examples permit temperament and preference to influence commitment where evidence is incomplete.
full: James makes philosophical belief answerable to concrete experience, yet his existential examples permit temperament and preference to influence commitment where evidence is incomplete. Pragmatism can therefore appear both experimentally disciplined and personally voluntarist.

kernel: Verification explains how propositions are tested, but equating truth with verification risks confusing the means of establishing a claim with what makes the claim true.
full: Verification explains how propositions are tested, but equating truth with verification risks confusing the means of establishing a claim with what makes the claim true.

kernel: A belief may produce its anticipated consequences because it reorganizes behavior rather than because it accurately describes an independent reality.
full: A belief may produce its anticipated consequences because it reorganizes behavior rather than because it accurately describes an independent reality. Pragmatic success can become performative self-confirmation.

kernel: Dewey rejects thought as passive representation, but inquiry still requires some adequate representation of the situation being transformed.
full: Dewey rejects thought as passive representation, but inquiry still requires some adequate representation of the situation being transformed. Reconstruction without sufficiently accurate description can intensify the original problem.

kernel: Instrumentalism judges concepts by efficacy relative to an end, but ends themselves require evaluation.
full: Instrumentalism judges concepts by efficacy relative to an end, but ends themselves require evaluation. An efficient instrument can serve a narrow, destructive, or unjust purpose.

kernel: The essay gives the individual mind a privileged role as the vehicle of experimental change while also locating individuals inside natural and social environments.
full: The essay gives the individual mind a privileged role as the vehicle of experimental change while also locating individuals inside natural and social environments. It underdevelops how institutions determine whose experiments are permitted and whose consequences count as evidence.

kernel: Dewey describes intelligence as the guarantee of a desirable future, but intelligence cannot determine desirability by itself.
full: Dewey describes intelligence as the guarantee of a desirable future, but intelligence cannot determine desirability by itself. Conflicts over values, interests, and power cannot always be resolved as technical problems.

kernel: Deleuze rejects the claim that cinema is a language, yet constructs an elaborate taxonomy of images and signs.
full: Deleuze rejects the claim that cinema is a language, yet constructs an elaborate taxonomy of images and signs. The project resists linguistic reduction while retaining the classificatory ambition of a grammar.

kernel: The claim that image, matter, light, and movement are identical is philosophically generative but difficult to operationalize.
full: The claim that image, matter, light, and movement are identical is philosophically generative but difficult to operationalize. It moves quickly between physical reality, perception, cinematic apparatus, and metaphysical ontology.

kernel: The acentred universe supposedly contains no privileged viewpoint, yet the theory depends upon living centres whose intervals organize perception and action.
full: The acentred universe supposedly contains no privileged viewpoint, yet the theory depends upon living centres whose intervals organize perception and action. The centre is rejected cosmologically and reintroduced biologically.

kernel: Perception as subtraction explains selective attention, but it can understate constructive processes such as memory, expectation, fantasy, language, and cultural interpretation.
full: Perception as subtraction explains selective attention, but it can understate constructive processes such as memory, expectation, fantasy, language, and cultural interpretation.

kernel: The face is made the privileged vehicle of affect, although affect can also be organized through posture, rhythm, sound, landscape, colour, texture, architecture, and collective movement.
full: The face is made the privileged vehicle of affect, although affect can also be organized through posture, rhythm, sound, landscape, colour, texture, architecture, and collective movement.

kernel: The action-image is described as realism because milieus and behaviours actualize one another.
full: The action-image is described as realism because milieus and behaviours actualize one another. Yet many highly stylized, fantastic, and melodramatic films also sustain this structure. “Realism” names a functional circuit more than a representational style.

kernel: The large-form action-image risks universalizing a particularly American narrative ideology: <Situation> → <Individual_Action> → <Reformed_World>.
full: The large-form action-image risks universalizing a particularly American narrative ideology: <Situation> → <Individual_Action> → <Reformed_World>. This structure can naturalize individual agency while marginalizing institutional, collective, systemic, or irresolvable conditions.

kernel: Deleuze borrows Peirce’s Firstness, Secondness, and Thirdness but transforms them into cinematic image-types.
full: Deleuze borrows Peirce’s Firstness, Secondness, and Thirdness but transforms them into cinematic image-types. The resulting system is productive, though it should not be mistaken for a straightforward application of Peirce’s semiotics.

kernel: The historical passage from movement-image to time-image is associated with war, neorealism, and the collapse of established schemas.
full: The historical passage from movement-image to time-image is associated with war, neorealism, and the collapse of established schemas. This creates a compelling periodization but can obscure earlier films that already broke sensory-motor continuity and later films that continue classical action structures.

kernel: The crisis of effective action is treated as a source of aesthetic invention.
full: The crisis of effective action is treated as a source of aesthetic invention. Politically, however, incapacitation, disorientation, and detachment may signify domination or trauma rather than liberation from cliché.

kernel: The taxonomy claims not to impose a hierarchy, yet the movement toward the direct time-image can appear evolutionary, as though classical cinema were a stage that modern cinema philosophically surpasses.
full: The taxonomy claims not to impose a hierarchy, yet the movement toward the direct time-image can appear evolutionary, as though classical cinema were a stage that modern cinema philosophically surpasses.

kernel: The definition of Mitchell’s imagetext as a single latent embedding is too literal and technologically specific.
full: The definition of Mitchell’s imagetext as a single latent embedding is too literal and technologically specific. Mitchell’s concept concerns the constitutive entanglement of verbal and visual media; it does not require that they be encoded as one computational vector. `Mitchell_WJT_Iconology_Image_Text_Ideology.pdf`

kernel: Multimodal systems do not necessarily store text and images as two reversible projections of one identical latent point.
full: Multimodal systems do not necessarily store text and images as two reversible projections of one identical latent point. Architectures may contain separate tokenizers, encoders, cross-attention systems, diffusion latents, alignment layers, and lossy mappings.

kernel: The claim that “holding the words is holding the parameters” overstates controllability.
